messages.content: Where and when was the 1930 FIFA World Cup held?
The 1930 FIFA World Cup took place in Uruguay from 13 to 30 July 1930, with all matches played in the capital, Montevideo.
Why was Uruguay selected to host the 1930 FIFA World Cup?

FIFA selected Uruguay as the host nation because the country was celebrating the centenary of its first constitution and because the Uruguay national team had retained their football title at the 1928 Summer Olympics.
How many teams entered the 1930 FIFA World Cup, and from which regions?
Thirteen teams entered the 1930 FIFA World Cup: seven from South America, four from Europe, and two from North America.
Who won the 1930 FIFA World Cup and what was the score in the final?
Uruguay won the 1930 FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina 4–2 in the final to become the first nation to win the World Cup.
Who scored the first goal in FIFA World Cup history at the 1930 tournament?
Lucien Laurent of France scored the first goal in World Cup history at the 1930 tournament.
Which four European teams ultimately made the trip to the 1930 FIFA World Cup?
The four European teams that made the trip by sea to the 1930 FIFA World Cup were Belgium, France, Romania, and Yugoslavia.
Why did so few European teams participate in the 1930 FIFA World Cup?

Few European teams participated because of the long, costly trip by ship across the Atlantic and the lengthy absence required of players, with the effects of the Great Depression setting in across the continent. No European entries were received before the February deadline, and FIFA president Jules Rimet had to intervene to secure four European teams.
What was the official attendance and result of the 1930 FIFA World Cup final?
The 1930 World Cup final was played at the Estadio Centenario on 30 July with an official attendance of 93,000, and Uruguay defeated Argentina 4–2.
Which four teams won their groups to qualify for the semi-finals at the 1930 FIFA World Cup?
Argentina, Yugoslavia, Uruguay, and the United States won their respective groups to qualify for the semi-finals at the 1930 FIFA World Cup.
Who scored the first hat-trick in the 1930 FIFA World Cup, and who recognized it?

Bert Patenaude of the United States scored the first tournament hat-trick, against Paraguay. FIFA acknowledged his claim as the first hat-trick scorer in 2006, after a goal previously assigned to teammate Tom Florie was reattributed to Patenaude.
What was notable about the Estadio Centenario at the 1930 FIFA World Cup?

The Estadio Centenario was built for the tournament and as a celebration of the centenary of Uruguayan independence. Designed by Juan Scasso and called a temple of football by Rimet, it had a capacity of 90,000 and was the largest football stadium outside the British Isles, hosting 10 of the 18 matches including both semi-finals and the final.
Who was the last surviving player from the 1930 FIFA World Cup?
Francisco Varallo of Argentina was the last surviving player from the 1930 FIFA World Cup; he died in 2010 at the age of 100.
How were the teams organized in the format of the 1930 FIFA World Cup?

The 13 teams were drawn into four groups, with Group 1 containing four teams and the others containing three. Each group played a round-robin format, and the four group winners advanced to the knockout semi-final stage.
What were the scores of the two semi-finals at the 1930 FIFA World Cup?
Both semi-finals at the 1930 FIFA World Cup ended with identical 6–1 scores: Argentina beat the United States 6–1, and Uruguay beat Yugoslavia 6–1.
How many goals were scored in total at the 1930 FIFA World Cup?
There were 70 goals scored in 18 matches at the 1930 FIFA World Cup, for an average of 3.89 goals per match.
Why was there no qualification for the 1930 FIFA World Cup?

The 1930 FIFA World Cup was the only one without qualification because every country affiliated with FIFA was invited to compete and the number of teams entering failed to reach 16, so no qualifying matches were needed.
Summarize what made the 1930 FIFA World Cup historically significant.

The 1930 FIFA World Cup was the first FIFA World Cup and the only one without qualification. Held in Uruguay, it saw the host nation become the first World Cup champions by beating Argentina 4–2 in the final. It featured the first World Cup goal by Lucien Laurent, the first clean sheet, the first hat-trick by Bert Patenaude, and remains the only final contested between two Spanish-speaking and two South American sides.
Which stadiums were used at the 1930 FIFA World Cup?
Three stadiums in Montevideo were used at the 1930 FIFA World Cup: the Estadio Centenario, the Estadio Pocitos, and the Estadio Gran Parque Central.
Where and when was the 1934 FIFA World Cup held?
The 1934 FIFA World Cup took place in Italy from 27 May to 10 June 1934.
Who won the 1934 FIFA World Cup and against whom?
Italy won the 1934 FIFA World Cup, beating Czechoslovakia 2–1 to become the second World Cup champions and the first European winners.
What was new about qualification at the 1934 FIFA World Cup?
The 1934 World Cup was the first in which teams had to qualify to take part. Thirty-two nations entered the competition and 16 qualified for the final tournament.
Why did reigning champions Uruguay boycott the 1934 FIFA World Cup?

Uruguay boycotted the 1934 FIFA World Cup in protest at the refusal of several European countries to travel to South America for the 1930 tournament, which Uruguay had hosted. This makes 1934 the only World Cup in which the reigning champions did not participate.
How was the 1934 FIFA World Cup connected to fascist politics?

The 1934 World Cup was a high-profile instance of a sporting event used for political gain, as Benito Mussolini was keen to use it to promote fascism. Some historians have accused Mussolini of corruption and meddling to benefit Italy, though Italy has always claimed it deserved its victory.
How was Italy chosen as host of the 1934 FIFA World Cup?

Italy was chosen as host at a FIFA executive committee meeting in Stockholm on 9 October 1932, after a lengthy process in which the committee met eight times. The decision was made without a ballot of members, and the Italian bid was preferred over one from Sweden.
What playing format was used at the 1934 FIFA World Cup?

The 1934 FIFA World Cup used a straight knockout tournament, discarding the group stage of the first World Cup. Tied matches went to 30 minutes of extra time, and if still level, were replayed the next day.
Which African team made its debut at the 1934 FIFA World Cup?
Egypt was the first team from Africa to play in a World Cup finals, qualifying for the 1934 tournament. It would not qualify again until the competition was next held in Italy, in 1990.
What was unusual about Italy's status as host of the 1934 FIFA World Cup?
Despite being hosts, Italy were still required to qualify for the 1934 FIFA World Cup, the only time the host nation needed to do so.
Who was the top scorer of the 1934 FIFA World Cup?
Oldřich Nejedlý was the top scorer of the 1934 FIFA World Cup with five goals.
What happened in the quarter-final between Italy and Spain at the 1934 FIFA World Cup?

Italy and Spain drew 1–1 after extra time, resulting in the first replayed match in World Cup history. The match was highly aggressive, injuring Spanish goalkeeper Ricardo Zamora and breaking the leg of Italy's Mario Pizziolo. Italy won the replay 1–0.
How did the British Home Nations respond to the 1934 FIFA World Cup?

The British Home Nations, in self-imposed exile from FIFA, refused to participate even though FIFA had offered England and Scotland direct entry without qualification. FA committee member Charles Sutcliffe called the tournament a joke.
What was notable about the last eight teams at the 1934 FIFA World Cup?

For the only time in World Cup history, the last eight at the 1934 tournament consisted entirely of European teams: Austria, Czechoslovakia, Germany, Hungary, Italy, Spain, Sweden, and Switzerland. All four non-European teams were eliminated after one match.
How many total goals were scored at the 1934 FIFA World Cup?
In total, 70 goals were scored at the 1934 FIFA World Cup by 45 players, with none credited as an own goal.
Which ball was used at the 1934 FIFA World Cup?
The Federale 102 ball, manufactured in Italy, was the match ball provided for the 1934 FIFA World Cup.
Where and when was the 1938 FIFA World Cup held?
The 1938 FIFA World Cup was held in France from 4 to 19 June 1938.
Who won the 1938 FIFA World Cup and what was the final score?
Italy won the 1938 FIFA World Cup, defending its title by beating Hungary 4–2 in the final.
What distinction do Italy's 1934 and 1938 World Cup teams hold?
Italy's 1934 and 1938 teams are the only men's national team to win the World Cup multiple times under the same coach, Vittorio Pozzo.
How was France chosen to host the 1938 FIFA World Cup?
France was chosen as host by FIFA in Berlin on 13 August 1936, selected over Argentina and Germany in the first round of voting.
Why did the choice of France as host of the 1938 FIFA World Cup cause controversy?
Holding a second consecutive tournament in Europe, after Italy in 1934, caused outrage in South America, where it was believed the venue should alternate between the two continents.
Which teams from outside Europe took part in the 1938 FIFA World Cup?

Only three non-European nations took part in the 1938 FIFA World Cup: Brazil, Cuba (making their debut), and Dutch East Indies (now Indonesia, also debuting), the latter being the first Asian country to participate in a World Cup. This was the smallest ever number of teams from outside the host continent at a World Cup.
What happened to Austria's place at the 1938 FIFA World Cup?

Austria qualified but, after the Anschluss united Austria with Germany, did not take part. Some Austrian players joined the German squad, but star player Matthias Sindelar refused to play for the unified team. Austria's place remained empty and Sweden progressed directly to the second round by default.
Which two teams qualified automatically for the 1938 FIFA World Cup?
The 1938 FIFA World Cup was the first time the hosts, France, and the title holders, Italy, qualified automatically. Title holders were given automatic entry from 1938 until 2002.
Why was the 1938 FIFA World Cup the last for 12 years?

The 1938 FIFA World Cup was the last for 12 years because the 1942 and 1946 World Cups were cancelled due to World War II. As a result, Italy were the reigning World Cup holders for a record 16 years, from 1934 to 1950.
Who was the top scorer of the 1938 FIFA World Cup?
Leônidas was the top scorer of the 1938 FIFA World Cup with seven goals.
How did Italy reach the final of the 1938 FIFA World Cup?
Italy beat the hosts France along the way, then faced Brazil in the semi-finals. With Brazil missing their injured star Leônidas, Italy won 2–1 to reach the final.
How did the 1934 knockout format carry over to the 1938 FIFA World Cup?

The 1938 FIFA World Cup retained the knockout format from 1934, with 30 minutes of extra time for tied matches and a replay if still level. It was the last World Cup tournament to use a straight knockout format.
How did Dr. Ottorino Barassi protect the World Cup trophy during the war after the 1938 tournament?
The Italian Vice-president of FIFA, Dr. Ottorino Barassi, hid the trophy in a shoe-box under his bed throughout the Second World War, saving it from falling into the hands of occupying troops.
How many goals were scored in total at the 1938 FIFA World Cup?
In total, 84 goals were scored at the 1938 FIFA World Cup by 42 players, with two of them credited as own goals.
Where and when was the 1950 FIFA World Cup held?
The 1950 FIFA World Cup was held in Brazil from 24 June to 16 July 1950.
Who won the 1950 FIFA World Cup and how was it decided?
Uruguay won the 1950 FIFA World Cup by defeating the host nation Brazil in the deciding match of the four-team final group. It was the only World Cup tournament not decided by a one-match final.
What is the Maracanaço, as it relates to the 1950 FIFA World Cup?

The Maracanaço (also called the Maracanazo, or Maracanã blow) refers to Uruguay's shocking defeat of host nation Brazil in the deciding match of the 1950 final group, considered one of the biggest upsets in football history.
Why had the World Cup not been staged for over 12 years before the 1950 edition?
The World Cup had not been staged since 1938 because the planned 1942 and 1946 World Cups were both cancelled due to World War II.
How did Brazil come to host the 1950 FIFA World Cup?

With FIFA struggling to find an interested host after the war, Brazil presented a bid at the 1946 FIFA Congress, offering to host the event on the condition that it take place in 1950 rather than the originally proposed 1949. The bid was quickly accepted.
What happened to defending champions Italy at the 1950 FIFA World Cup?

Italy, the two-time defending champions, were eliminated in the first round for the first time in their history. Their team had been severely weakened, as most of its starting line-up perished in the Superga air disaster a year before the tournament, and they travelled by boat rather than by plane.
Why was the 1950 FIFA World Cup the first to call the trophy the Jules Rimet Cup?
The 1950 FIFA World Cup was the inaugural tournament where the trophy was referred to as the Jules Rimet Cup, to mark the 25th anniversary of Jules Rimet's presidency of FIFA.
How many teams ultimately competed at the 1950 FIFA World Cup?
Although 16 teams originally qualified, withdrawals left the 1950 FIFA World Cup with only thirteen teams, resulting in just three nations in Group 3 and two in Group 4.
Which teams advanced to the final group stage of the 1950 FIFA World Cup?
The final group stage of the 1950 FIFA World Cup involved Brazil, Spain, Sweden, and Uruguay, the teams that had won their first-round groups.
Why did England's 1950 FIFA World Cup campaign end early?
England, one of the favourites, crashed out of the 1950 FIFA World Cup after a shock 1–0 defeat by the United States and a 1–0 defeat by Spain.
What was the score and key moment of the deciding 1950 FIFA World Cup match between Brazil and Uruguay?

Brazil needed only a draw to win the title and went ahead through Friaça early in the second half, but Uruguay equalised and then Alcides Ghiggia scored past Moacyr Barbosa with just over 11 minutes left, giving Uruguay a 2–1 win and their second World Cup title.
Why did Brazil propose a new playing format for the 1950 FIFA World Cup?

The Brazilian organisers proposed a two-round group format to maximise matches and ticket sales, since the stadiums and infrastructure had been costly. The format guaranteed 30 games rather than the 16 a straight knockout would have provided, and each team at least three games. FIFA agreed after Brazil threatened to back out of hosting.
What was the attendance at the deciding 1950 FIFA World Cup match at the Maracanã?
The deciding match between Brazil and Uruguay on 16 July was watched by a huge home crowd of 199,954, with some estimates as high as 205,000, at the Estádio do Maracanã.
Who was the top scorer of the 1950 FIFA World Cup?
Brazil's Ademir was the top scorer of the 1950 FIFA World Cup with nine goals.
Which team was the only debutant at the 1950 FIFA World Cup?
Of the thirteen teams that competed at the 1950 FIFA World Cup, only England was making its debut.
How many venues and matches were there at the 1950 FIFA World Cup?
Six venues in six cities around Brazil hosted the 22 matches of the 1950 FIFA World Cup, with the Maracanã in Rio de Janeiro hosting eight matches.
What happened to Brazil goalkeeper Moacyr Barbosa after the 1950 FIFA World Cup?

Barbosa was blamed for Brazil's defeat in the deciding match and suffered for the rest of his life, as the failure to win at home became part of Brazilian culture. In 2000, shortly before his death, he said he had been paying for 50 years for something he was not even responsible for.
Summarize why the 1950 FIFA World Cup was unique among World Cup tournaments.

The 1950 FIFA World Cup was the first held in over 12 years after the war-cancelled 1942 and 1946 editions, and it was the only tournament not decided by a one-match final, instead using a final round-robin group. It featured the elimination of defending champions Italy in the first round, only 13 competing teams after withdrawals, and Uruguay's famous Maracanaço upset of host Brazil.
Who won the 1954 FIFA World Cup?
West Germany won the 1954 FIFA World Cup, defeating tournament favourites Hungary 3-2 in the final for their first World Cup title.
Which country hosted the 1954 FIFA World Cup?
Switzerland hosted the 1954 FIFA World Cup, which was held from 16 June to 4 July, having been selected as host country in July 1946.
What was the final score of the 1954 FIFA World Cup final?
West Germany beat Hungary 3-2 in the 1954 FIFA World Cup final, with Helmut Rahn scoring the winning goal.
What is the 1954 FIFA World Cup final known as in Germany?
West Germany's victory in the 1954 final is known in Germany as the 'Miracle of Bern' (Das Wunder von Bern), and was memorialised in a 2003 film of the same name.
Who was the top scorer at the 1954 FIFA World Cup?
Sándor Kocsis of Hungary was the top scorer at the 1954 FIFA World Cup with 11 goals, bettering the previous record by three goals.
What was the highest-scoring match of the 1954 FIFA World Cup?
The highest-scoring match was the quarter-final in which Austria defeated hosts Switzerland 7-5; those 12 goals have never been surpassed in a men's World Cup.
What goalscoring records were set at the 1954 FIFA World Cup?

The 1954 FIFA World Cup set the all-time record for highest average goals per game at 5.38, and Hungary set records including most goals scored by a team (27) and highest average goals scored per game (5.4).
What format was used at the 1954 FIFA World Cup?

The 1954 tournament used a unique format in which sixteen teams were divided into four groups of four, each group containing two seeded and two unseeded teams, with only four matches per group pitting a seeded team against an unseeded team; the top two teams from each group progressed to the knockout stage.
What was the Battle of Berne at the 1954 FIFA World Cup?
The Battle of Berne was the quarter-final in which favourites Hungary beat Brazil 4-2 in one of the most violent matches in football history.
How did Hungary reach the 1954 FIFA World Cup final?
Hungary reached the final by beating Brazil 4-2 in the quarter-finals and Uruguay 4-2 in the semi-final after extra time, with Sándor Kocsis scoring two late goals against Uruguay.
What controversies surrounded the 1954 FIFA World Cup final?

The 1954 final remains controversial due to apparent refereeing errors, including a disallowed Puskás equaliser ruled offside, and later allegations of doping involving claims the West German players were injected with substances at half-time.
What was notable about the West German team's footwear in the 1954 FIFA World Cup final?
Adi Dassler of Adidas provided the West German team with a new kind of shoes featuring exchangeable studs that were replaced during halftime of the 1954 final, which was played in the rain.
How did West Germany's path to winning the 1954 FIFA World Cup make history?

West Germany became the first team to win the World Cup after having lost a match at the finals, having lost 8-3 to Hungary in the group stage when they fielded a reserve side, and it remains the only time a team won the World Cup with amateur footballers.
How did Uruguay perform at the 1954 FIFA World Cup?
Uruguay, the defending champions, were eliminated by Hungary in the semi-final and then lost to Austria in the match for third place, finishing fourth.
How many goals were scored at the 1954 FIFA World Cup?
In total, 140 goals were scored by 63 players at the 1954 FIFA World Cup, with four of them credited as own goals.
How many venues hosted the 1954 FIFA World Cup?
Six venues in six cities hosted the 26 matches of the 1954 FIFA World Cup, with the St. Jakob Stadium in Basel being the most used, hosting six matches.
Who won the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
Brazil won the 1958 FIFA World Cup, beating hosts Sweden 5-2 in the final in Solna, Stockholm, to claim their first title.
Which country hosted the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
Sweden hosted the 1958 FIFA World Cup, which was played from 8 to 29 June 1958, and it remains the only FIFA World Cup hosted in a Nordic country.
What was the final score of the 1958 FIFA World Cup final?
Brazil beat Sweden 5-2 in the 1958 FIFA World Cup final, after Sweden had gone a goal up after four minutes.
Who was the top scorer at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
Just Fontaine of France was the top scorer at the 1958 FIFA World Cup with 13 goals, setting the record for most goals ever scored in a single World Cup tournament, a record that still stands.
How did Pelé feature at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?

The 1958 tournament marked the arrival of a then 17-year-old Pelé on the world stage; he scored in all three of Brazil's knockout games, including a hat-trick against France in the semi-final, and became the youngest player to participate in, score in, and win a World Cup final.
Which teams made their World Cup debut at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
Wales, Northern Ireland and the Soviet Union made their FIFA World Cup debuts at the 1958 tournament.
What was unique about the Home Nations at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?

The 1958 FIFA World Cup was the only one to date to feature all four of the United Kingdom's Home Nations, with Wales and Northern Ireland making their debuts and England and Scotland also qualifying.
What format was used at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?

Sixteen teams competed in four groups of four, with each team playing every other team in its group at least once without extra time in the event of a draw; two points were awarded for a win and one for a draw, and the top two teams advanced.
How were the groups seeded at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?

Seeding at the 1958 FIFA World Cup was geographical rather than by team strength, with each group containing one western European team, one eastern European team, one of the four British teams, and one team from the Americas.
Which teams did Brazil beat on the way to winning the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
Brazil beat Wales in the quarter-final and France in the semi-final before defeating Sweden in the final to win the 1958 FIFA World Cup.
How did West Germany perform at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
Defending champions West Germany were eliminated by runners-up Sweden in the semi-final, losing 3-1, and then lost 6-3 to France in the match for third place.
What World Cup first occurred when England and Brazil met at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
When England and Brazil drew 0-0 at the 1958 FIFA World Cup, it was the first time in World Cup history that a game ended goalless.
What records were set in the 1958 FIFA World Cup final?

The final featured the highest number of goals scored by a winning team (5) and the highest total goals scored (7); Pelé became the youngest player to play in, score in, and win a final, while Nils Liedholm became the oldest player to score in a World Cup final at 35 years 263 days.
How many goals were scored at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
In total, 126 goals were scored by 60 players at the 1958 FIFA World Cup, with none credited as an own goal.
Which stadium hosted the most matches at the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
The Råsunda Stadium in Stockholm was the most used venue at the 1958 FIFA World Cup, hosting 8 matches including the final.
What was significant about Italy and the 1958 FIFA World Cup?
The 1958 FIFA World Cup was the first one for which Italy failed to qualify, having taken part in every previous edition that had qualification.
Where and when was the 1962 FIFA World Cup held?
The 1962 FIFA World Cup was held in Chile from 30 May to 17 June 1962.
Which team won the 1962 FIFA World Cup and who did they beat in the final?
Brazil won the 1962 FIFA World Cup, defeating Czechoslovakia 3–1 in the final in Santiago.
Which edition of the FIFA World Cup was the 1962 tournament?
The 1962 FIFA World Cup was the seventh edition of the FIFA World Cup.
Which team finished third at the 1962 FIFA World Cup?
Host nation Chile finished third at the 1962 FIFA World Cup, defeating Yugoslavia 1–0 in the match for third place.
What was the violent match known as the Battle of Santiago at the 1962 World Cup?

The Battle of Santiago was the first-round match between host Chile and Italy, which Chile won 2–0, and it became notorious for repeated attempts by players on both sides to harm opponents, with two Italian players sent off and the Italian team needing police protection to leave the field.
What significant goalscoring milestone occurred at the 1962 FIFA World Cup?
The 1962 FIFA World Cup was the first World Cup in which the average number of goals per match was less than three, at 2.78, a trend that has been repeated at every World Cup since.
How did Brazil's win at the 1962 World Cup compare to past achievements?
By winning in 1962, Brazil became the second team, after Italy in 1934 and 1938, to win the World Cup twice consecutively, a feat no team has achieved since.
Which cities were chosen to host the 1962 World Cup before the earthquake forced changes?
Originally eight stadiums were selected in eight cities: Santiago, Viña del Mar, Rancagua, Arica, Talca, Concepción, Talcahuano and Valdivia.
How did the 1960 Valdivia earthquake affect the 1962 World Cup preparations?

The Valdivia earthquake of 22 May 1960, the most powerful ever recorded, caused over 50,000 casualties and forced the organising committee to modify the calendar, with Talca, Concepción, Talcahuano and Valdivia severely damaged and discarded as venues.
Who were the top scorers of the 1962 FIFA World Cup and how many goals did they score?
With four goals each, Flórián Albert, Garrincha, Valentin Ivanov, Dražan Jerković, Leonel Sánchez and Vavá were the top scorers of the 1962 tournament.
How many teams qualified for the 1962 World Cup final tournament and how were they organised?
Sixteen teams qualified for the 1962 World Cup, divided into four groups of four, with the top two teams in each group advancing to the quarter-finals.
Which team became the first to be eliminated on goal average in World Cup history, and at which tournament?
At the 1962 World Cup, Argentina became the first team in World Cup history to be eliminated on goal average, when England advanced from Group 4 in second place due to a superior goal average.
Which phrase did Carlos Dittborn coin during Chile's bid for the 1962 World Cup?
Carlos Dittborn coined the phrase "Because we have nothing, we want to do it all" (Porque no tenemos nada, queremos hacerlo todo) during Chile's bid to host the 1962 World Cup.
How many votes did Chile receive to win hosting rights for the 1962 World Cup?
Chile won the hosting rights for the 1962 World Cup with 32 votes to Argentina's 10, with fourteen members abstaining.
Who scored Brazil's goals in the 1962 World Cup final?
In the 1962 World Cup final, Brazil's goals were scored by Amarildo, Zito and Vavá, in their 3–1 win over Czechoslovakia.
Summarise the playing style trend seen at the 1962 FIFA World Cup.

At the 1962 FIFA World Cup a shift in strategy occurred as defensive strategies began to take hold, dropping the average goals per match to 2.78, under three for the first time in competition history, a level the average has never exceeded since.
Why did Pelé not play in the 1962 World Cup final?
Pelé was injured in Brazil's second group match against Czechoslovakia, and he was replaced by Amarildo for the remainder of the 1962 tournament, including the final.
Where and when was the 1966 FIFA World Cup played?
The 1966 FIFA World Cup was played in England from 11 to 30 July 1966.
Who won the 1966 FIFA World Cup and what was the final score?
England won the 1966 FIFA World Cup, defeating West Germany 4–2 in the final to win their first and only World Cup title.
What did Geoff Hurst achieve in the 1966 World Cup final?
Geoff Hurst scored a hat-trick in the 1966 World Cup final, the first hat-trick ever scored in a World Cup final, with two of his goals coming in extra time.
Who was the top scorer at the 1966 FIFA World Cup?
Portuguese striker Eusébio was the top scorer at the 1966 FIFA World Cup with nine goals, earning him the golden boot, three goals ahead of second-placed Helmut Haller.
What happened to defending champions Brazil at the 1966 World Cup?
Two-time reigning champions Brazil failed to get past the group stage at the 1966 World Cup, as they were defeated by Hungary and Portugal.
How did North Korea perform at the 1966 FIFA World Cup?
North Korea, one of the debut teams, beat Italy 1–0 on the way to reaching the quarter-finals, where they lost to Portugal 5–3 after leading 3–0.
Why did African nations boycott the 1966 FIFA World Cup?

All fifteen entered African nations boycotted the 1966 World Cup to protest a 1964 FIFA ruling that required the three African second-round winners to enter a play-off against the Asian zone winners to qualify; they considered winning their own zone sufficient to merit qualification.
What was the story of Pickles the dog at the 1966 World Cup?

Before the 1966 World Cup the Jules Rimet trophy was stolen from an exhibition display, and it was recovered by a dog named Pickles, who found it wrapped in newspaper under bushes in a London garden four months before the tournament began.
Which stadium hosted the 1966 World Cup final and how many people attended?
London's Wembley Stadium hosted the 1966 World Cup final, which was attended by 98,000 people.
How was England chosen to host the 1966 FIFA World Cup?

England was chosen as host of the 1966 World Cup in Rome, Italy, on 22 August 1960, over rival bids from West Germany and Spain, and it was the first tournament held in a country directly affected by World War II.
What was the mascot of the 1966 FIFA World Cup?

The mascot of the 1966 FIFA World Cup was "World Cup Willie", a cartoon lion wearing a Union Jack jersey, which was the first World Cup mascot and one of the first mascots associated with a major sporting competition.
What disciplinary reforms followed the controversies of the 1966 FIFA World Cup?

Following the violent matches and contentious dismissals at the 1966 World Cup, FIFA introduced the yellow and red card system ahead of the 1970 World Cup, along with allowing two substitutions per team and efforts to standardise referee training and interpretations of the Laws of the Game.
Who became the first player sent off in a senior international match at Wembley during the 1966 World Cup?

Argentina's Antonio Rattín became the first player to be sent off in a senior international football match at Wembley, during the controversial 1966 quarter-final against England, when he was dismissed by referee Rudolf Kreitlein.
Which team finished third at the 1966 FIFA World Cup?
Portugal finished third at the 1966 FIFA World Cup, beating the Soviet Union 2–1 in the bronze match, the best finish by a team making its World Cup debut since 1934.
How many venues were used at the 1966 World Cup and which was the most used?
Eight venues were used at the 1966 World Cup, and the most used was Wembley Stadium, which hosted nine matches including all six of England's games, the final, and the third-place match.
What broadcasting milestone is associated with the 1966 FIFA World Cup final?

The 1966 World Cup final, broadcast locally by the BBC, was the last World Cup final to be shown entirely in black and white, and the 1966 tournament was the first to have selected matches broadcast via satellite to other continents.
How did the goals unfold in the 1966 World Cup final between England and West Germany?

Helmut Haller put West Germany ahead after about 12 minutes, Geoff Hurst equalised four minutes later, Martin Peters gave England the lead in the 78th minute, and Wolfgang Weber equalised for West Germany in the final minute to force extra time, after which Hurst scored twice more to complete his hat-trick and seal England's 4–2 win.
Where and when was the 1970 FIFA World Cup held?
The 1970 FIFA World Cup was held in Mexico from 31 May to 21 June 1970, the first World Cup held outside Europe and South America and the first hosted in North America.
Who won the 1970 FIFA World Cup and what was the score in the final?
Brazil won the 1970 FIFA World Cup, defeating Italy 4–1 in the final.
What did Brazil's third World Cup title in 1970 allow them to do with the trophy?
Brazil's third World Cup title in 1970 allowed them to permanently keep the Jules Rimet Trophy, and a new trophy was introduced in 1974.
Who was the top scorer at the 1970 FIFA World Cup?
Gerd Müller was the top scorer at the 1970 FIFA World Cup with 10 goals.
Which teams made their debut at the 1970 FIFA World Cup finals?
El Salvador, Israel and Morocco made their debut appearances at the 1970 World Cup final stage.
What goalscoring record was set at the 1970 FIFA World Cup?

Despite the issues of altitude and high temperature, the 1970 World Cup finals largely produced attacking football and set an average goals per game record that has not since been bettered by any subsequent World Cup finals.
Why is the Brazilian team of the 1970 World Cup considered so significant?

The 1970 Brazilian team, led by Carlos Alberto and featuring players such as Pelé, Gérson, Jairzinho, Rivellino and Tostão, is often cited as the greatest football team of all time, achieving a perfect record of wins in all six finals games as well as all their qualifying fixtures.
What was significant about Anatoliy Puzach at the 1970 FIFA World Cup?
Anatoliy Puzach of the Soviet Union became the first substitute to be used in FIFA World Cup history, coming on after half-time in the opening match against Mexico.
What famous save did Gordon Banks make at the 1970 World Cup?

At the 1970 World Cup, Gordon Banks in the English goal denied Pelé from close range with a reflex save that Pelé himself cited as the greatest of his career, during the group match between Brazil and England.
How was Mexico chosen as host of the 1970 FIFA World Cup?
Mexico was chosen as host of the 1970 World Cup by a vote at the FIFA congress in Tokyo on 8 October 1964, where the only other bid was submitted by Argentina.
Which match at the 1970 World Cup became known as the "Game of the Century"?

The semi-final between Italy and West Germany became known as the "Game of the Century"; Italy won 4–3 after extra time produced five goals, and a plaque outside the Estadio Azteca commemorates the match.
What feat did Jairzinho accomplish at the 1970 FIFA World Cup?
Jairzinho scored in every one of Brazil's matches at the 1970 World Cup, a feat that has yet to be equalled.
How many goals did Brazil score in the 1970 World Cup final and who scored them?
Brazil scored four goals in the 1970 World Cup final, from Pelé, Gérson, Jairzinho and Carlos Alberto, securing a 4–1 victory over Italy.
What card system was introduced for the first time at the 1970 FIFA World Cup?
The 1970 FIFA World Cup was the first finals at which referees could issue yellow and red cards, although no player was actually expelled from play during the tournament.
What was the conflict known as the Football War connected to the 1970 World Cup qualification?
El Salvador qualified for the 1970 finals after beating Honduras in a play-off, which was the catalyst for a four-day conflict in July 1969 known as the Football War.
What match ball was used for the first time at the 1970 FIFA World Cup?

The 1970 FIFA World Cup was the first to use the Telstar ball from Adidas, which was the first World Cup ball to use the truncated icosahedron design of 12 black pentagonal and 20 white hexagonal panels.
What achievement did coach Mário Zagallo reach at the 1970 FIFA World Cup?
With Brazil's 1970 victory, Mário Zagallo became the first man to win the World Cup both as a player, in 1958 and 1962, and as a coach.
Where and when was the 1974 FIFA World Cup played?
The 1974 FIFA World Cup was played in West Germany, including West Berlin, between 13 June and 7 July 1974.
Who won the 1974 FIFA World Cup and what was the final score?
West Germany won the 1974 FIFA World Cup, beating the Netherlands 2–1 in the final at the Olympiastadion in Munich.
What was significant about the trophy awarded at the 1974 FIFA World Cup?

The 1974 FIFA World Cup marked the first time that the current trophy, the FIFA World Cup Trophy created by Italian sculptor Silvio Gazzaniga, was awarded, following Brazil's permanent retention of the Jules Rimet Trophy in 1970.
Who was the top scorer at the 1974 FIFA World Cup?
Grzegorz Lato of Poland was the top scorer at the 1974 FIFA World Cup with seven goals.
Which teams made their first World Cup finals appearance at the 1974 tournament?

Australia, East Germany, Haiti and Zaire made their first finals appearances at the 1974 FIFA World Cup, with Haiti and Zaire also making their only appearance and East Germany making their only appearance as a separate team before German reunification.
What scoring record did Gerd Müller break in the 1974 World Cup final?
Gerd Müller's goal in the 1974 final was the 14th of his World Cup career across two tournaments, beating Just Fontaine's record of 13 goals; Müller's record stood until 2006.
What new tournament format was introduced at the 1974 FIFA World Cup?

The 1974 FIFA World Cup featured a new format in which the eight teams advancing from the four first-round groups did not enter a knockout stage but instead played in a second group stage, with the two group winners meeting in the final; this format was used only in 1974 and 1978.
How was West Germany chosen to host the 1974 FIFA World Cup?

West Germany was chosen by FIFA to host the 1974 World Cup in London on 6 July 1966, partly through a deal in which Spain supported West Germany for 1974 in return for West Germany allowing Spain to bid for the 1982 World Cup unopposed.
Which playing style did the Netherlands showcase at the 1974 FIFA World Cup?

At the 1974 FIFA World Cup the Netherlands demonstrated the "Total Football" techniques pioneered by the club Ajax, in which specialised positions were virtually abolished and outfield players became defenders, midfielders or strikers as the situation required.
What happened when the two German teams met at the 1974 FIFA World Cup?

In a first-ever clash between the two German teams at the 1974 World Cup, East Germany won 1–0 thanks to a late Jürgen Sparwasser goal, in one of the most politically charged matches of all time, although West Germany had already secured progression to the second round.
Who became the first player to be sent off with a red card in a World Cup match, and when?
Carlos Caszely of Chile became the first player to be sent off with a red card in a World Cup match, during Chile's match against West Germany at the 1974 World Cup.
How did the penalties unfold in the 1974 World Cup final?

In the 1974 final, the Netherlands took the lead within a minute through a Johan Neeskens penalty before any German player had touched the ball, and West Germany equalised with a Paul Breitner penalty in the 26th minute; these were the first two penalties awarded in a World Cup final.
Who scored the winning goal in the 1974 World Cup final?
Gerd Müller scored the winning goal in the 1974 World Cup final in the 43rd minute, the last goal of his international career, giving West Germany a 2–1 victory over the Netherlands.
Why was Scotland's elimination from the 1974 FIFA World Cup notable?

Scotland were eliminated from the 1974 World Cup on goal difference despite not losing a game, making them the only team that did not lose a match in the tournament and the first ever country eliminated from a World Cup finals without having lost a match.
What were the official mascots of the 1974 FIFA World Cup?
The official mascots of the 1974 FIFA World Cup were Tip and Tap, two boys wearing an outfit similar to West Germany's, with the letters WM and the number 74.
Why was the USSR disqualified during qualification for the 1974 FIFA World Cup?

The USSR was controversially disqualified during qualification for the 1974 World Cup after refusing to travel for the second leg of their playoff against Chile, as a result of the 1973 Chilean coup d'état.
What record margin of victory was equalled at the 1974 FIFA World Cup?
Yugoslavia hammered Zaire 9–0 at the 1974 World Cup, equalling the finals record for the largest margin of victory.
Where and when was the 1978 FIFA World Cup held?
The 1978 FIFA World Cup was held in Argentina between 1 and 25 June 1978.
Who won the 1978 FIFA World Cup and what was the final score?
Host nation Argentina won the 1978 FIFA World Cup, defeating the Netherlands 3–1 after extra time in the final.
Which edition of the FIFA World Cup was the 1978 tournament?
The 1978 FIFA World Cup was the 11th edition of the FIFA World Cup.
Where was the 1978 FIFA World Cup final held?
The 1978 FIFA World Cup final was held at River Plate's home stadium, the Estadio Monumental, in Buenos Aires.
Who was the top scorer at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
Mario Kempes of Argentina was the top scorer at the 1978 FIFA World Cup with six goals.
What was significant about Argentina's win at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
The 1978 win was Argentina's first World Cup title, making them the fifth team to be both hosts and world champions and the third South American team to win the World Cup.
Which teams made their first appearance at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
Iran and Tunisia made their first appearances at the 1978 FIFA World Cup.
What happened to defending champions West Germany at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
Defending champions West Germany were eliminated in the second round of the 1978 FIFA World Cup, finishing third in their group.
How many teams competed at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
The 1978 FIFA World Cup was the last tournament to use the format of 16 teams, before FIFA expanded the competition to 24 teams for the 1982 edition in Spain.
What was the format of the 1978 FIFA World Cup?

The 1978 FIFA World Cup had 16 teams divided into four groups of four playing round-robin, with the top two from each group advancing to a second round of two groups of four; the winners of those groups met in the final and the runners-up in the third-place match.
How was Argentina chosen to host the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
Argentina was chosen as host of the 1978 FIFA World Cup by FIFA on 6 July 1966 in London, when the hosts for the 1974, 1978 and 1982 editions were all chosen.
What controversy surrounded the 1978 FIFA World Cup?

The 1978 FIFA World Cup was marred by controversy, domestic politics, and alleged interference and match-fixing by the Argentine military junta, which used the tournament for nationalistic propaganda and to seek legitimacy on the world stage.
What was notable about Tunisia at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
Tunisia made history at the 1978 FIFA World Cup by beating Mexico 3–1 after trailing 0–1 at half time, the first time any African team had won a match at the World Cup finals.
Which milestone goal was scored by Rob Rensenbrink at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
Rob Rensenbrink's goal against Scotland at the 1978 FIFA World Cup was the 1000th goal of World Cup history.
What controversy surrounded Argentina's 6–0 win over Peru at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?

Argentina needed to beat Peru by four clear goals to reach the final and won 6–0 after Peru collapsed in the second half, prompting rumours that Peru might have been bribed or threatened, though nothing could be proved.
What was the mascot of the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
The official mascot of the 1978 FIFA World Cup was Gauchito, a boy wearing an Argentina kit, with a hat reading ARGENTINA '78, a neckerchief and a whip typical of gauchos.
Which was the largest and most used venue at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
The River Plate Stadium in Buenos Aires, with a capacity of 74,624, was the largest and most used venue at the 1978 FIFA World Cup, hosting nine matches including the final.
How many goals were scored at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
In total, 102 goals were scored by 62 players at the 1978 FIFA World Cup, with three of them credited as own goals.
Who scored Argentina's goals in the 1978 FIFA World Cup final?
Mario Kempes opened the scoring and added a second goal, and Daniel Bertoni also scored, as Argentina beat the Netherlands 3–1 after extra time in the 1978 final.
What happened to Scotland's Willie Johnston at the 1978 FIFA World Cup?
Scotland's Willie Johnston was expelled from the 1978 FIFA World Cup after he was found to have taken a banned stimulant during the opening game against Peru.
Where and when was the 1982 FIFA World Cup played?
The 1982 FIFA World Cup was played in Spain from 13 June to 11 July 1982.
Who won the 1982 FIFA World Cup and what was the final score?
Italy won the 1982 FIFA World Cup, defeating West Germany 3–1 in the final.
Which edition of the FIFA World Cup was the 1982 tournament?
The 1982 FIFA World Cup was the 12th FIFA World Cup.
Where was the 1982 FIFA World Cup final held?
The 1982 FIFA World Cup final was held at the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium in Madrid.
What was significant about Italy's 1982 World Cup title?
The 1982 title was Italy's third World Cup title, but their first since 1938, and it equalled Brazil's record of winning the World Cup three times.
Who received the Golden Boot at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
Paolo Rossi received the Golden Boot at the 1982 FIFA World Cup for scoring six goals.
How many teams competed at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
The 1982 FIFA World Cup expanded the finals from 16 to 24 teams for the first time, allowing more countries from Africa and Asia to participate.
Which teams made their first World Cup finals appearance at the 1982 tournament?
Algeria, Cameroon, Honduras, Kuwait and New Zealand made their first appearances in the finals at the 1982 FIFA World Cup.
What happened to defending champions Argentina at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
Defending champions Argentina were eliminated in the second round of the 1982 FIFA World Cup, finishing third and last in their group.
What was the format of the 1982 FIFA World Cup?

The 1982 FIFA World Cup began with a first round of six groups of four teams, with the top two in each advancing to a second round of four groups of three; the winner of each second-round group progressed to the knockout semi-finals.
What penalty shootout milestone occurred at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
The 1982 FIFA World Cup featured the first penalty shootout in World Cup competition, in the semi-final between France and West Germany.
What was the 'Disgrace of Gijón' at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?

The 'Disgrace of Gijón' refers to the final Group 2 match between West Germany and Austria, in which after an early West German goal both teams played out the rest of the match aimlessly because a 1–0 or 2–0 German win would qualify them both at Algeria's expense; this led FIFA to play the final two games in each group simultaneously at later tournaments.
What major upset occurred in Group 2 at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
In one of the great World Cup upsets, newcomers Algeria beat reigning European Champions West Germany 2–1 on the first day of Group 2 at the 1982 FIFA World Cup.
What record-equalling scoreline occurred at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
In the first round of Group 3, Hungary defeated El Salvador 10–1, equalling the largest margin of victory recorded in the finals.
What was notable about Harald Schumacher's foul at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?

West German goalkeeper Harald Schumacher's foul on French player Patrick Battiston in the Seville semi-final left Battiston unconscious with a broken jaw and two teeth knocked out, yet the referee deemed it not to be a foul; it has been described as one of history's most shocking fouls.
How did the France versus West Germany semi-final end at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
The 1982 semi-final between France and West Germany finished 3–3 after extra time, and West Germany won the resulting penalty shootout 5–4, the first shootout at a World Cup finals.
Which player marked Diego Maradona closely at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
Italian player Claudio Gentile carried out intense and often violent match-long marking of Argentine player Diego Maradona at the 1982 FIFA World Cup.
Which team finished third at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
Poland finished third at the 1982 FIFA World Cup, edging France 3–2 in the third-place match, matching their best previous World Cup performance from 1974.
Which was the most used venue at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
FC Barcelona's Camp Nou stadium was the most used venue at the 1982 FIFA World Cup, hosting five matches including a semi-final, and it was the largest stadium used with a capacity of 121,401.
How many goals were scored at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
In total, 146 goals were scored by 100 players at the 1982 FIFA World Cup, with only one of them credited as an own goal.
What record did 40-year-old Dino Zoff set at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
Italy's 40-year-old captain-goalkeeper Dino Zoff became the oldest player to win the World Cup at the 1982 FIFA World Cup.
What was notable about Italy's path to the title at the 1982 FIFA World Cup?
Italy became the first team to advance from the first round without winning a game, drawing all three matches, and the first World Cup winner to draw or lose three matches at the finals.
Which country hosted the 1986 FIFA World Cup, and what made its selection historically notable?
The 1986 FIFA World Cup was hosted by Mexico, which became the first country to host the World Cup more than once, having previously hosted the 1970 edition.
Who won the 1986 FIFA World Cup and who did they beat in the final?
Argentina won the 1986 FIFA World Cup, beating West Germany 3-2 in the final at Mexico City's Estadio Azteca. It was their second title, after winning in 1978.
Why was Colombia unable to host the 1986 FIFA World Cup?

Colombia had originally been chosen to host the 1986 World Cup, but largely due to economic reasons it was unable to do so and resigned in November 1982, as the next Colombian president, Belisario Betancur, declared they could not afford to host under the terms FIFA demanded.
Who captained Argentina at the 1986 FIFA World Cup and how old was he?
Argentina was captained by the 25-year-old Diego Maradona at the 1986 FIFA World Cup.
What were the two famous goals Maradona scored against England in the 1986 FIFA World Cup quarter-final?

In the 1986 quarter-final against England, Maradona scored his 'Hand of God' goal, which he punched into the net past goalkeeper Peter Shilton, and another goal voted the 'Goal of the Century,' in which he dribbled half the length of the field past five English players before scoring.
How many goals did Maradona score during the 1986 FIFA World Cup, and how many did he create for teammates?
Maradona scored five goals during the 1986 FIFA World Cup and also created another five for his teammates.
When and where was the 1986 FIFA World Cup played?
The 1986 FIFA World Cup was played in Mexico from 31 May to 29 June 1986.
Which three teams made their first appearances at the final stage of the 1986 FIFA World Cup?
Canada, Denmark and Iraq made their first appearances at the final stage of the 1986 FIFA World Cup.
Which team became the first African team to reach the second round at the 1986 FIFA World Cup?
Morocco became the first African team to reach the second round, topping Group F and becoming only the second nation from outside Europe and the Americas to do so, after North Korea in 1966.
Who won the Golden Boot at the 1986 FIFA World Cup and how many goals did he score?
Gary Lineker of England won the Golden Boot as the leading scorer of the 1986 World Cup with six goals.
Who won the Golden Ball at the 1986 FIFA World Cup?
Maradona was the Golden Ball winner as the best player of the 1986 FIFA World Cup.
What was the total attendance at the 1986 FIFA World Cup and the average per match?
Total attendance at the 1986 FIFA World Cup was 2,394,031, an average per match of 46,039.
What spectator phenomenon was popularised worldwide during the 1986 FIFA World Cup?
The Mexican wave was a spectator phenomenon popularised worldwide after featuring during the 1986 FIFA World Cup.
Who were the defending champions at the 1986 FIFA World Cup and how were they eliminated?
Italy were the defending champions at the 1986 FIFA World Cup, but they were eliminated by France in the Round of 16.
What was the official match ball of the 1986 FIFA World Cup and who manufactured it?

The official match ball of the 1986 FIFA World Cup was the 'Azteca,' manufactured by Adidas. It was the first fully synthetic FIFA World Cup ball, with a design inspired by the hosting nation's native Aztec architecture and murals.
What was the official mascot of the 1986 FIFA World Cup?

The official mascot of the 1986 World Cup was Pique, a jalapeno pepper with a moustache, a sombrero, and Mexican football team colours, whose name comes from the Spanish word 'picante' meaning 'spicy' and was a pun on the 'PK' abbreviation for penalty kick.
Explain how France and Brazil's 1986 FIFA World Cup quarter-final was decided.

In the 1986 quarter-final, France and Brazil were level after Careca and Michel Platini scored, and Brazil's Zico had a penalty saved by Joel Bats. The match went to extra time with no further goals, so it was decided by a penalty shoot-out. Socrates and Julio Cesar missed for Brazil while Platini fired over the bar, and Luis Fernandez scored to put France through 4-3 on penalties.
Summarize the format of the 1986 FIFA World Cup.

The 1986 FIFA World Cup was the second tournament to feature a 24-team format. The 24 teams were divided into six groups of four (A to F), with the final pair of matches in each group starting at the same time. The top two teams and the four best third-place finishers from the six groups advanced to a knockout round of 16, after which the competition was played on a knockout basis rather than in groups.
How did the 1986 FIFA World Cup final unfold after Argentina took a 2-0 lead?

In the 1986 final, Jose Brown put Argentina one up and Jorge Valdano scored a second in the 55th minute, but West Germany staged a comeback through Karl-Heinz Rummenigge in the 74th minute and Rudi Voller's equaliser six minutes later. With seven minutes remaining, a pass from Maradona gave Jorge Burruchaga the chance to score the winner for Argentina.
Which country hosted the 1990 FIFA World Cup and when was it held?
The 1990 FIFA World Cup was held in Italy from 8 June to 8 July 1990, making Italy the second country to host the event for a second time, after Mexico in 1986.
Who won the 1990 FIFA World Cup and who did they beat in the final?
Germany won the 1990 FIFA World Cup for the third time, beating defending champions Argentina 1-0 at the Stadio Olimpico in Rome, a rematch of the previous final four years earlier.
How was the only goal of the 1990 FIFA World Cup final scored?

The only goal of the 1990 final came in the 85th minute when Mexican referee Edgardo Codesal awarded a penalty to Germany after a foul on Rudi Voller by Roberto Sensini, and Andreas Brehme converted the spot kick to settle the contest.
Which teams finished third and fourth at the 1990 FIFA World Cup?
Italy finished third and England fourth at the 1990 FIFA World Cup, after both lost their semi-finals in penalty shootouts.
What did Cameroon achieve at the 1990 FIFA World Cup?
Cameroon went on an unexpectedly strong run at the 1990 FIFA World Cup, becoming the first African team to reach the quarter-finals of the World Cup.
Who was Roger Milla and what role did he play for Cameroon at the 1990 FIFA World Cup?

Roger Milla was a 38-year-old forward who came out of international retirement to join Cameroon's squad at the last moment after a personal request from Cameroonian President Paul Biya. His four goals and flamboyant goal celebrations made him one of the tournament's biggest stars and helped take Cameroon to the last eight.
What scoring record did the 1990 FIFA World Cup set?
The 1990 World Cup generated an average of 2.21 goals per game, a record low that still stands, as well as a then-record 16 red cards, including the first dismissal in a final.
Which three teams made their first appearances in the finals at the 1990 FIFA World Cup?
Costa Rica, the Republic of Ireland and the United Arab Emirates made their first appearances in the finals at the 1990 FIFA World Cup.
Why was the 1990 FIFA World Cup the last tournament to feature a West Germany team?
The 1990 FIFA World Cup was the last tournament to feature a team from West Germany because the country was reunified with East Germany a few months later in October 1990.
Who won the Golden Boot at the 1990 FIFA World Cup?
Salvatore Schillaci of Italy received the Golden Boot at the 1990 FIFA World Cup for scoring six goals, making him the second Italian footballer to win it after Paolo Rossi in 1982.
What rule changes resulted from the defensive play seen at the 1990 FIFA World Cup?

The overly defensive tactics at the 1990 World Cup led to the introduction of the back-pass rule in time for 1994 and the awarding of three points for a win instead of two, both intended to encourage attacking play.
How did England's semi-final against Germany at the 1990 FIFA World Cup end?
The semi-final between Germany and England at the 1990 World Cup ended 1-1, with Gary Lineker equalising for England, and Germany won the penalty shoot-out 4-3.
Which famous musical performance featured during the 1990 FIFA World Cup?
Luciano Pavarotti performed 'Nessun dorma' during the first ever joint concert of The Three Tenors on July 7, shortly before the match for third place at the 1990 FIFA World Cup.
How many teams entered the 1990 FIFA World Cup and how many qualified?
Teams representing 116 national football associations entered the 1990 FIFA World Cup, and 22 teams qualified through the process, along with host nation Italy and defending champions Argentina.
What distinction did manager Franz Beckenbauer earn by winning the 1990 FIFA World Cup?

German manager Franz Beckenbauer became the first man to both captain (in 1974) and manage a World Cup winning team, and only the second man after Mario Zagallo of Brazil to win the World Cup as a player and as team manager.
What was the official mascot of the 1990 FIFA World Cup?
The official mascot of the 1990 FIFA World Cup was 'Ciao,' a stick figure player with a football head and an Italian tricolore body, whose name is an Italian greeting.
Why did England play all their group stage matches in Cagliari at the 1990 FIFA World Cup?

At the British government's request, England played all three group stage matches in Cagliari on the island of Sardinia because of concerns about hooliganism among English fans, which had spilled over onto the European continent during the 1980s, putting the English Football Association's reputation and even UK-Italy diplomatic relations at risk.
Summarize Cameroon's group stage campaign at the 1990 FIFA World Cup.

At the 1990 World Cup, Cameroon defeated world champions Argentina 1-0 in their opening match despite finishing with nine men, then beat Romania 2-1 with two goals from Roger Milla off the bench, and topped the group despite a 4-0 defeat to the Soviet Union in their final group game.
What unwanted firsts did Argentina set in the 1990 FIFA World Cup final?

In the 1990 final, Argentina's Pedro Monzon became the first player ever sent off in a World Cup Final, and Argentina became the first team to fail to score in a World Cup Final. They were also reduced to nine men after Gustavo Dezotti received a second yellow card.
Which country hosted the 1994 FIFA World Cup and when was it held?
The 1994 FIFA World Cup was hosted by the United States and took place from June 17 to July 17, 1994, at nine venues across the country.
Who won the 1994 FIFA World Cup and how was the final decided?

Brazil won the 1994 FIFA World Cup, defeating Italy 3-2 in a penalty shootout at the Rose Bowl in Pasadena, California, after the game had ended 0-0 after extra time. It was the first World Cup final to be decided on penalties.
What milestone did Brazil reach by winning the 1994 FIFA World Cup?
The victory at the 1994 FIFA World Cup made Brazil the first nation to win four World Cup titles.
Why was the 1994 FIFA World Cup considered the most financially successful in World Cup history?

The 1994 FIFA World Cup broke tournament records with overall attendance of 3,587,538 and an average of 68,991 per game, figures that remained unsurpassed despite the later expansion of the competition from 24 to 32 teams.
Who missed the decisive penalty in the 1994 FIFA World Cup final?

Roberto Baggio of Italy, playing injured, had to score to keep Italy's hopes alive in the 1994 final shootout, but he missed by shooting it over the crossbar, and Brazil were crowned champions for the fourth time.
Which three teams made their World Cup debuts at the 1994 FIFA World Cup?

Greece, Nigeria, and Saudi Arabia were the three new entrants who made their debuts at the 1994 FIFA World Cup. Russia also appeared as a separate nation for the first time following the breakup of the Soviet Union.
What rule changes were introduced at the 1994 FIFA World Cup to encourage attacking play?

At the 1994 FIFA World Cup, three points were awarded for a win instead of two, a relaxed offside rule was used, and there was a ban on picking up back-passes to goalkeepers. These changes raised the average to 2.71 goals per match, compared to 2.21 in 1990.
What happened to Diego Maradona at the 1994 FIFA World Cup?
Diego Maradona was expelled from the 1994 FIFA World Cup by FIFA after he failed a drug test that uncovered ephedrine, a weight-loss drug, in his blood. He played only Argentina's first two games.
What happened to Colombian defender Andres Escobar after the 1994 FIFA World Cup?

After scoring an own goal against the United States that effectively eliminated Colombia from the 1994 competition, defender Andres Escobar was shot to death on his return to Colombia outside a bar in a Medellin suburb 10 days later, possibly in retaliation for his mistake.
How did Bulgaria perform at the 1994 FIFA World Cup?

Bulgaria was one of the biggest surprises of the 1994 World Cup, reaching the semi-finals despite never having won a game in five previous appearances. Led by Hristo Stoichkov, they beat Mexico on penalties and reigning champions Germany 2-1 in the quarter-finals before finishing fourth after losses to Italy and Sweden.
Who shared the Golden Boot at the 1994 FIFA World Cup?
The Golden Boot at the 1994 FIFA World Cup went jointly to Bulgaria's Hristo Stoichkov and Oleg Salenko of Russia, both of whom scored six goals.
What record did Oleg Salenko set at the 1994 FIFA World Cup?
Oleg Salenko of Russia became the first player to score five goals in a single World Cup game, in a 6-1 victory against Cameroon at the 1994 FIFA World Cup.
Who won the Golden Ball at the 1994 FIFA World Cup?
Brazilian striker Romario, with five goals, won the Golden Ball as the best player of the 1994 FIFA World Cup.
What record did Roger Milla set at the 1994 FIFA World Cup?
Roger Milla, at the age of 42, became the oldest World Cup goalscorer of all time when he scored Cameroon's consolation goal in their 6-1 loss to Russia at the 1994 FIFA World Cup.
How did the host nation United States fare at the 1994 FIFA World Cup?

The host nation United States advanced to the second round as one of the best third-place teams at the 1994 FIFA World Cup, but were eliminated in the Round of 16 in a 1-0 defeat to Brazil on Independence Day.
Why was Group E unique in the history of the 1994 FIFA World Cup?
Group E at the 1994 FIFA World Cup remains the only group in World Cup history in which all four teams finished with the same points, and all four teams even had the same goal difference.
What was the official mascot of the 1994 FIFA World Cup?

The official mascot of the 1994 FIFA World Cup was 'Striker, the World Cup Pup,' a dog wearing a red, white and blue soccer uniform with a ball, designed by the Warner Bros. animation team. A dog was picked because dogs are a popular domestic pet in the United States.
What notable refereeing first occurred at the 1994 FIFA World Cup involving an Italian goalkeeper?
Gianluca Pagliuca of Italy became the first goalkeeper to be sent off in a World Cup game, dismissed for handling outside his area against Norway at the 1994 FIFA World Cup.
Summarize how Roberto Baggio carried Italy to the final of the 1994 FIFA World Cup.

At the 1994 FIFA World Cup, Roberto Baggio scored the tying goal and then a penalty in the Round of 16 against Nigeria to send Italy through, the game-winning goal in the quarter-final against Spain, and both goals in Italy's semi-final victory over Bulgaria, carrying the Italians to the final.
Who won the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
Host country France won the 1998 FIFA World Cup, their first title, becoming the seventh nation to win a World Cup.
Where and when was the 1998 FIFA World Cup held?
The 1998 FIFA World Cup was held in France from 10 June to 12 July 1998, spanning 32 days.
What was the score of the 1998 FIFA World Cup final?
France beat defending champions Brazil 3–0 in the 1998 FIFA World Cup final at the Stade de France in Saint-Denis.
Who scored in the 1998 FIFA World Cup final for France?
France's goals in the 1998 final came from two headers by Zinedine Zidane and a stoppage-time strike from Emmanuel Petit.
How many teams competed at the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
The 1998 FIFA World Cup was the first to expand the group stage from 24 to 32 teams, arranged in eight groups of four.
Who won the Golden Boot at the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
Davor Šuker of Croatia received the Golden Boot at the 1998 FIFA World Cup for scoring six goals.
Which teams made their first appearance at the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
Croatia, Jamaica, Japan and South Africa made their first World Cup appearances at the 1998 tournament.
How many matches and stadiums were used at the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
64 matches were played in 10 stadiums across 10 host cities at the 1998 FIFA World Cup, with the opening match and final staged at the newly built Stade de France.
How did Croatia finish at the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
Croatia finished third at the 1998 FIFA World Cup, beating the Netherlands in the third-place play-off, with Davor Šuker scoring the winner in the 36th minute.
What controversy surrounded the 1998 FIFA World Cup host selection?

In 2015, Chuck Blazer confirmed that members of FIFA's executive committee were bribed during the 1998 host selection process; it eventually transpired that the bribe payment came from the failed Moroccan bid, not the winning French bid.
What new rules were used for the first time at the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
The 1998 FIFA World Cup was the first since the introduction of golden goals, the banning of dangerous tackles from behind, and the allowance of three substitutions per game.
What happened to Marcel Desailly in the 1998 FIFA World Cup final?
France went down to ten men in the 68th minute of the 1998 final when Marcel Desailly was sent off for a second bookable offence.
Which team did France defeat in the 1998 final, and what was the significance for Brazil?
France defeated Brazil 3–0 in the 1998 final, inflicting what was then the second-heaviest World Cup defeat on Brazil, later topped by Brazil's 7–1 loss to Germany in 2014.
How many goals were scored at the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
A total of 171 goals were scored by 112 players at the 1998 FIFA World Cup.
What was the official match ball of the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
The official match ball of the 1998 FIFA World Cup was the 'Tricolore', manufactured by Adidas, and it was the first World Cup ball in the series to be multi-coloured.
What was the official mascot of the 1998 FIFA World Cup?

The official mascot of the 1998 FIFA World Cup was Footix, a rooster created by graphic designer Fabrice Pialot, whose name is a portmanteau of 'football' and the '-ix' ending from the Astérix comic strip.
Who became the youngest and oldest players selected at the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
Samuel Eto'o of Cameroon was the youngest player selected at 17 years and 3 months, while Jim Leighton of Scotland was the oldest at 39 years and 11 months.
Who performed the official song of the 1998 FIFA World Cup?
The official song of the 1998 FIFA World Cup was 'The Cup of Life', also known as 'La Copa de la Vida', recorded by Ricky Martin.
Who won the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
Brazil won the 2002 FIFA World Cup, becoming the first and only country to have won the World Cup five times.
Where was the 2002 FIFA World Cup held?
The 2002 FIFA World Cup was held from 31 May to 30 June 2002 at sites in Japan and South Korea, the first World Cup held in Asia and the first jointly hosted by more than one nation.
What was the score of the 2002 FIFA World Cup final?
Brazil won the 2002 FIFA World Cup final against Germany 2–0, with both goals scored by Ronaldo.
Where was the 2002 FIFA World Cup final held?
The 2002 FIFA World Cup final was hosted by Japan at International Stadium in Yokohama.
Who won the Golden Shoe at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
Ronaldo of Brazil won the Golden Shoe at the 2002 FIFA World Cup as the tournament's leading scorer with eight goals.
How far did South Korea progress at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?

South Korea reached the semi-finals at the 2002 FIFA World Cup, beating Poland, Portugal, Italy and Spain en route, becoming the first team from outside the UEFA, CONMEBOL and CONCACAF regions to reach a World Cup semi-final.
Which teams made their World Cup debut at the 2002 tournament?
China, Ecuador, Senegal and Slovenia made their World Cup debuts in 2002, with Senegal being the only debutant to advance from the group stage and reach the quarter-finals.
What notable upset happened to France at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
Defending champions France were eliminated in the group stage of the 2002 FIFA World Cup after earning a single point without scoring a goal, including a 1–0 loss to Senegal in the opening match.
Who finished third at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
Turkey finished third at the 2002 FIFA World Cup, beating South Korea 3–2 in the third-place match, in only their second ever FIFA World Cup.
What scoring record was set at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
At the 2002 FIFA World Cup, Turkey's Hakan Şükür scored the fastest goal in World Cup history, just 10.8 seconds after kick-off, in the third-place match against South Korea.
How were the hosts of the 2002 FIFA World Cup selected?

South Korea and Japan were chosen unanimously as co-hosts of the 2002 FIFA World Cup on 31 May 1996, in preference to Mexico, after UEFA and the AFC viewed co-hosting between the two Asian rivals as the best option.
How were the venues split between the hosts at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
South Korea and Japan each provided 10 venues for the 2002 FIFA World Cup, with Groups A–D playing all their matches in South Korea and Groups E–H playing all their matches in Japan.
Which group was the 'group of death' at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
Group F was considered the group of death at the 2002 FIFA World Cup, as it brought together Argentina, England, Nigeria and Sweden.
What record did Brazil's Cafu set at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
Brazil's captain Cafu became the first player to appear in three successive World Cup finals when he accepted the trophy on behalf of the team in 2002.
What controversies arose over refereeing at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?

There was much controversy over refereeing at the 2002 FIFA World Cup, with questionable decisions in the Italy–South Korea match resulting in 400,000 complaints, and two controversially disallowed Spanish goals in the Spain–South Korea match.
How many goals were scored at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
A total of 161 goals were scored by 109 players at the 2002 FIFA World Cup, with three credited as own goals.
What was the official match ball of the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
The official match ball of the 2002 FIFA World Cup was the Fevernova, manufactured by Adidas.
What were the official mascots of the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
The official mascots of the 2002 FIFA World Cup were Ato, Kaz and Nik, known as the Spheriks, futuristic CGI creatures coloured orange, purple and blue respectively.
Who won the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
Italy won the 2006 FIFA World Cup, claiming their fourth World Cup title.
Where and when was the 2006 FIFA World Cup held?
The 2006 FIFA World Cup was held from 9 June to 9 July 2006 in Germany, the second time Germany staged the competition.
How was the 2006 FIFA World Cup final decided?
Italy defeated France 5–3 in a penalty shoot-out in the 2006 final, after extra time had finished in a 1–1 draw.
Who scored in normal time in the 2006 FIFA World Cup final?
Zinedine Zidane opened the scoring for France with a seventh-minute penalty, and Marco Materazzi levelled the scores for Italy in the 19th minute following an Andrea Pirlo corner.
What happened to Zinedine Zidane in the 2006 FIFA World Cup final?
Near the end of extra time in the 2006 final, Zinedine Zidane head-butted Marco Materazzi in the chest in an off-the-ball incident and was sent off.
Who finished third at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
Germany finished third at the 2006 FIFA World Cup, defeating Portugal 3–1 in the third-place play-off.
Who won the Golden Boot at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
Germany's Miroslav Klose won the Golden Boot at the 2006 FIFA World Cup with five goals, the lowest total to win the prize since 1962.
Which teams made their first appearance at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
Angola, Ukraine, Ghana, the Ivory Coast, Trinidad and Tobago and Togo made their first FIFA World Cup appearances in 2006.
How were the hosts of the 2006 FIFA World Cup chosen?
The vote to host the 2006 FIFA World Cup was held in July 2000 in Zürich among Germany, South Africa, England and Morocco; after three rounds of voting, Germany won the final round over South Africa.
What was unusual about goalscoring at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
No player managed to score a hat-trick at the 2006 FIFA World Cup, the only time this happened in the tournament's history, and no player other than Miroslav Klose scored more than three goals.
What disciplinary records were set at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
The 2006 FIFA World Cup had a record number of cards, with players receiving a record-breaking 345 yellow cards and 28 red cards, breaking the previous record set by the 1998 World Cup.
What was the 'Battle of Nuremberg' at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?

The 'Battle of Nuremberg' was the round of 16 match between Portugal and the Netherlands at the 2006 FIFA World Cup, in which Russian referee Valentin Ivanov handed out 16 yellow cards and 4 red cards.
What happened to defending champions Brazil at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
Brazil were the defending world champions at the 2006 FIFA World Cup but were eliminated by France 1–0 in the quarter-finals.
What scoring milestone was reached at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?

For the first time ever in the FIFA World Cup, the first and last goals of the 2006 tournament were scored by defenders: Philipp Lahm scored the opener against Costa Rica, and Marco Materazzi headed in the last goal in the final.
How did the host nation Germany perform at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
Germany finished third at the 2006 FIFA World Cup; they were eliminated in the semi-finals by Italy in extra time and then defeated Portugal 3–1 in the third-place play-off.
How many goals were scored at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
A total of 147 goals were scored by 110 players at the 2006 FIFA World Cup, with four credited as own goals.
What were the Fan Fests at the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
Fan Fests were public viewing events set up in 12 host cities at the 2006 FIFA World Cup, giving supporters without tickets a way to take part; they attracted 21 million visitors over the tournament.
What was the official match ball and mascot of the 2006 FIFA World Cup?
The official match ball of the 2006 FIFA World Cup was the 'Teamgeist', manufactured by Adidas, and the official mascot was 'Goleo VI'.
Which country hosted the 2010 FIFA World Cup?
The 2010 FIFA World Cup was hosted by South Africa, which became the first African nation to host the tournament finals.
Who won the 2010 FIFA World Cup?
Spain won the 2010 FIFA World Cup, defeating the Netherlands 1–0 after extra time to claim their first world title.
When did the 2010 FIFA World Cup take place?
The 2010 FIFA World Cup took place in South Africa from 11 June to 11 July 2010.
Who scored the winning goal in the 2010 FIFA World Cup final?
Andrés Iniesta scored the winning goal for Spain in the 2010 FIFA World Cup final, netting in the 116th minute of extra time, the latest winning goal in a World Cup final.
Which edition of the FIFA World Cup was the 2010 tournament?
The 2010 FIFA World Cup was the 19th FIFA World Cup, the world championship for men's national football teams.
Where was the final of the 2010 FIFA World Cup played?
The final of the 2010 FIFA World Cup was held at Soccer City (FNB Stadium) in Johannesburg on 11 July 2010.
How many teams competed in the 2010 FIFA World Cup?
A total of 32 teams competed in the 2010 FIFA World Cup, drawn from six confederations.
How many goals were scored at the 2010 FIFA World Cup?
A total of 145 goals were scored at the 2010 FIFA World Cup, an average of 2.27 per match, the lowest of any World Cup since the tournament switched to a 64-game format.
Who finished third and fourth at the 2010 FIFA World Cup?
Germany finished third at the 2010 FIFA World Cup, defeating Uruguay 3–2 in the third-place match, while Uruguay finished fourth.
Which player won the Golden Ball at the 2010 FIFA World Cup?
Diego Forlán of Uruguay was named the best player of the 2010 FIFA World Cup, winning the Golden Ball.
Who were the top scorers of the 2010 FIFA World Cup?

Four players shared the top scorer honour at the 2010 FIFA World Cup with five goals each: Diego Forlán, Thomas Müller, Wesley Sneijder and David Villa, with the Golden Boot going to Thomas Müller of Germany on the basis of his three assists.
Explain how Spain's 2010 FIFA World Cup victory was historically significant.

Spain's 2010 FIFA World Cup win was significant because they became the eighth nation to win the tournament and the first European nation to win a World Cup hosted outside its home continent. They were also the first team to win the tournament after losing their opening game, and they set a record for the fewest goals scored by a World Cup-winning team, with eight.
What happened to host nation South Africa at the 2010 FIFA World Cup?

Host nation South Africa was eliminated in the group stage of the 2010 FIFA World Cup, becoming the first host nation in World Cup history to be eliminated in the first round, despite beating France and drawing with Mexico.
How was the host of the 2010 FIFA World Cup selected?

South Africa was selected to host the 2010 FIFA World Cup as part of a rotation policy that limited bidding to African nations, with FIFA president Sepp Blatter announcing the winning bid on 15 May 2004 in Zürich after South Africa received 14 votes to Morocco's 10 and Egypt's none in the first round of voting.
What was the official match ball of the 2010 FIFA World Cup?

The official match ball of the 2010 FIFA World Cup was the Adidas Jabulani, which means "bringing joy to everyone" in Zulu and featured 11 colours representing each player on the pitch and the 11 official languages of South Africa.
