What was the official mascot of the 2010 FIFA World Cup?

The official mascot of the 2010 FIFA World Cup was Zakumi, an anthropomorphised African leopard with green hair, whose name came from "ZA" (the abbreviation for South Africa) and "kumi", meaning "ten" in various African languages.
What was notable about the vuvuzela at the 2010 FIFA World Cup?

The 2010 FIFA World Cup amplified international awareness of the vuvuzela, a long horn blown by fans throughout matches, which drew complaints from players such as Patrice Evra and Lionel Messi and from broadcasters whose commentary was drowned out by the noise.
How much prize money was on offer at the 2010 FIFA World Cup and how much did the winner receive?
The total prize money for the 2010 FIFA World Cup was US$420 million, a 60 percent increase on 2006, with the winning team receiving US$30 million.
Which country hosted the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
The 2014 FIFA World Cup was hosted by Brazil, the second time the country staged the competition after 1950.
Who won the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
Germany won the 2014 FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina 1–0 after extra time to secure the country's fourth world title.
When did the 2014 FIFA World Cup take place?
The 2014 FIFA World Cup took place in Brazil from 12 June to 13 July 2014.
Who scored the winning goal in the 2014 FIFA World Cup final?
Mario Götze scored the winning goal for Germany in the 2014 FIFA World Cup final, a half-volley in the 113th minute of extra time.
Which edition of the FIFA World Cup was the 2014 tournament?
The 2014 FIFA World Cup was the 20th FIFA World Cup, the quadrennial world championship for men's national football teams organised by FIFA.
How many venues and host cities were used at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
The 2014 FIFA World Cup used 12 venues located in 12 host cities across Brazil.
What was significant about Brazil's semi-final at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
In the semi-finals of the 2014 FIFA World Cup, host nation Brazil lost 7–1 to Germany, the biggest defeat in Brazilian football since 1920, and Brazil eventually finished in fourth place.
Who was named the best player of the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
Lionel Messi of Argentina was named the best player of the 2014 FIFA World Cup, winning the Golden Ball.
Who won the Golden Boot at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
James Rodríguez of Colombia won the Golden Boot at the 2014 FIFA World Cup, scoring six goals, the first time a Colombian player received the award.
What new technologies were used for the first time at a World Cup finals in 2014?
At the 2014 FIFA World Cup, match officials used goal-line technology and vanishing spray for free kicks for the first time at a World Cup finals.
How many goals were scored at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
A total of 171 goals were scored at the 2014 FIFA World Cup by a record 121 players, an average of 2.67 goals per match.
What happened to defending champions Spain at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
Defending champions Spain were eliminated at the group stage of the 2014 FIFA World Cup after only two games, the quickest exit for defending champions since Italy in 1950.
Explain the significance of Germany's win at the 2014 FIFA World Cup for European football.

Germany's 2014 FIFA World Cup victory made them the first European team to win a World Cup staged in the Americas, and it marked the third consecutive title won by a European team after Italy in 2006 and Spain in 2010, taking Europe to eleven World Cup victories against South America's nine.
Which team made its World Cup debut at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
Bosnia and Herzegovina was the only debutant at the 2014 FIFA World Cup, joining 31 other qualified teams and the host nation Brazil.
What disciplinary case involving Luis Suárez occurred at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?

At the 2014 FIFA World Cup, Uruguayan striker Luis Suárez was suspended for nine international matches and banned from football-related activity for four months, and fined CHF100,000, following a biting incident on Italian defender Giorgio Chiellini.
What was the official match ball of the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
The official match ball of the 2014 FIFA World Cup was the Adidas Brazuca, which used a six-panel thermally bonded design after criticisms of the Jabulani used in the previous World Cup.
Who finished third at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
The Netherlands finished third at the 2014 FIFA World Cup, defeating Brazil 3–0 in the third-place match, the first third-place finish in Dutch history.
How did Miroslav Klose make history at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?

At the 2014 FIFA World Cup, Miroslav Klose scored his 16th World Cup goal in the semi-final against Brazil, breaking the all-time record he had previously shared with Ronaldo, and he also became the first player to appear in four World Cup semi-finals.
Which country hosted the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
The 2018 FIFA World Cup was hosted by Russia, the first time the championship was held in Eastern Europe.
Who won the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
France won the 2018 FIFA World Cup, defeating Croatia 4–2 in the final to claim their second World Cup title.
When and where was the 2018 FIFA World Cup final played?
The 2018 FIFA World Cup final was played on 15 July 2018 at the Luzhniki Stadium in Moscow, where France beat Croatia 4–2.
Which edition of the FIFA World Cup was the 2018 tournament?
The 2018 FIFA World Cup was the 21st FIFA World Cup, the quadrennial world championship for national football teams organised by FIFA.
How many teams and venues featured at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
The 2018 FIFA World Cup featured 32 teams, with 64 matches played in 12 venues across 11 cities in Russia.
Who won the Golden Ball at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
Croatian player Luka Modrić was voted the best player of the 2018 FIFA World Cup, winning the Golden Ball.
Who won the Golden Boot at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
England's Harry Kane won the Golden Boot at the 2018 FIFA World Cup as the tournament's top scorer with six goals.
Who won the Golden Glove at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
Belgium's Thibaut Courtois won the Golden Glove at the 2018 FIFA World Cup, awarded to the goalkeeper with the best performance.
What happened to defending champions Germany at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
Defending champions Germany were eliminated in the group stage of the 2018 FIFA World Cup for the first time since 1938.
Which teams made their World Cup debut at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
Iceland and Panama each made their World Cup debut at the 2018 FIFA World Cup, with Iceland becoming the smallest country by population to reach the World Cup.
Explain how VAR was used for the first time at the 2018 FIFA World Cup.

The 2018 FIFA World Cup was the first World Cup to use video assistant referees (VAR), approved by the FIFA Council after the IFAB incorporated VAR into the Laws of the Game in March 2018. VAR operations were run from a single headquarters in Moscow, with Diego Costa's goal against Portugal becoming the first World Cup goal based on a VAR decision.
Who finished third and fourth at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
Belgium finished third at the 2018 FIFA World Cup and England finished fourth.
How was Russia chosen to host the 2018 FIFA World Cup?

Russia was chosen to host the 2018 FIFA World Cup when the 22-member FIFA Executive Committee voted in Zürich on 2 December 2010, with Russia winning the right in the second round of voting ahead of the Portugal/Spain and Belgium/Netherlands bids, while England was eliminated in the first round.
How many goals were scored at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
There were 169 goals scored in 64 matches at the 2018 FIFA World Cup, an average of 2.64 goals per match, including a record 12 own goals.
Why was Russia's selection as host of the 2018 FIFA World Cup controversial?

Russia's selection as host of the 2018 FIFA World Cup was controversial because of issues including racism in Russian football, human rights abuses, discrimination against LGBT people, the annexation of Crimea, and allegations of corruption and bribery in the bidding process.
What was significant about Germany's World Cup victory streak that continued at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?

France's win at the 2018 FIFA World Cup made them the fourth consecutive title won by a European team, after Italy in 2006, Spain in 2010, and Germany in 2014, extending a record winning streak for teams from the same continent.
What was the official mascot of the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
The official mascot of the 2018 FIFA World Cup was Zabivaka, an anthropomorphic wolf dressed in the colours of the Russian national team, selected by public vote with about 1 million votes.
Which two teams played in the opening match of the 2018 FIFA World Cup?

Russia played Saudi Arabia in the opening match of the 2018 FIFA World Cup at the Luzhniki Stadium in Moscow on 14 June, a match between the two lowest-ranked teams of the tournament at the time of the final draw.
Which country hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup, the first World Cup held in the Middle East and the Arabian Peninsula, and the second held in an Asian country.
Who won the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup, defeating France 4–2 on penalties after a 3–3 draw following extra time, claiming their third title and first since 1986.
What was the result of the 2022 World Cup final?
In the 2022 World Cup final, Argentina and France drew 3–3 after extra time, and Argentina won 4–2 on penalties. The final was played on 18 December 2022 at Lusail Stadium.
Why was the 2022 World Cup held in November and December?

The 2022 World Cup was held in November and December to avoid the extremes of Qatar's hot and humid summer climate, making it the first World Cup to take place at the end of the year and outside the traditional months of May, June, and July.
Who finished third and fourth at the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
Croatia finished third at the 2022 FIFA World Cup after beating Morocco 2–1, while Morocco finished fourth, a record best finish for any African or Arab nation.
What historic achievement did Morocco accomplish at the 2022 World Cup?
At the 2022 World Cup, Morocco became the first African and first Arab nation to reach the semi-finals of a World Cup, falling 0–2 to France before finishing fourth overall.
Who was the top scorer at the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
Kylian Mbappé of France was the top scorer at the 2022 FIFA World Cup with eight goals, winning the Golden Boot.
What did Kylian Mbappé achieve in the 2022 World Cup final?

Kylian Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup final, the first hat-trick in a World Cup final since Geoff Hurst in 1966, and became the first player to score in two consecutive finals since Vavá in 1958 and 1962.
Who won the Golden Ball at the 2022 World Cup?
Argentine captain Lionel Messi was voted the best player of the 2022 World Cup, winning his second Golden Ball.
Who won the best young player and best goalkeeper awards at the 2022 World Cup?
Enzo Fernández of Argentina won the Young Player Award and teammate Emiliano Martínez won the Golden Glove as best goalkeeper at the 2022 World Cup.
How did host nation Qatar perform at the 2022 World Cup?

Qatar, making their World Cup debut, lost all three group matches and became the first host nation to lose their opening match and the first to be knocked out after just two games at the 2022 World Cup.
What was the biggest upset of the 2022 World Cup group stage?

Saudi Arabia's 2–1 win over Argentina was considered one of the biggest upsets in World Cup history, with second-half goals from Saleh Al-Shehri and Salem Al-Dawsari overturning an early Messi penalty.
How many goals were scored at the 2022 FIFA World Cup?

A total of 172 goals were scored in 64 matches at the 2022 FIFA World Cup, an average of 2.69 per match, the most ever scored at a FIFA World Cup and a record for the 32-team format, with every participating team scoring at least one goal.
How many teams and venues featured at the 2022 World Cup?
The 2022 World Cup featured 32 teams, the last edition with that number before expansion to 48, playing 64 matches across eight venues in five cities over a reduced 29-day timeframe.
What new substitution rules were used at the 2022 World Cup?

The 2022 World Cup featured new substitution rules allowing up to five substitutions in normal time plus an additional one in extra time, and it was the first World Cup to feature concussion substitutions.
What was significant about the referees at the 2022 World Cup?

For the first time, women referees officiated games at a major men's tournament at the 2022 World Cup, with Stéphanie Frappart of France, Salima Mukansanga of Rwanda, and Yoshimi Yamashita of Japan appointed. Frappart became the first to officiate a World Cup match in the Costa Rica vs Germany game.
What was the total attendance at the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
The 2022 FIFA World Cup drew a total attendance of 3,404,252, an average of 53,191 per match across the 64 games.
Why was Russia absent from the 2022 World Cup?
Russia, the previous World Cup hosts, were disqualified from competing at the 2022 World Cup due to the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
What controversies surrounded Qatar hosting the 2022 World Cup?

Hosting the 2022 World Cup in Qatar attracted significant criticism over the country's treatment of migrant workers, women, and members of the LGBTQ+ community, as well as its climate, lack of a strong football culture, scheduling changes, and allegations of bribery and FIFA corruption.
What record did Cristiano Ronaldo set at the 2022 World Cup?
By scoring a penalty against Ghana, Cristiano Ronaldo became the first man to score in five different World Cups at the 2022 tournament.
What was the estimated cost of the 2022 FIFA World Cup?

The 2022 FIFA World Cup was estimated to cost over $220 billion, making it the most expensive World Cup ever held, though Qatari officials disputed this figure, with organising CEO Nasser Al Khater saying the true cost was $8 billion.
What is the FIFA World Cup?

The FIFA World Cup is an international association football competition among the senior men's national teams of the members of FIFA, the sport's global governing body. It has been held every four years since the inaugural tournament in 1930, except in 1942 and 1946 due to World War II.
When and where was the first FIFA World Cup held?
The first FIFA World Cup was held in 1930 in Uruguay, which was named host to celebrate the centenary of its independence and its status as two-time official football world champions.
Who won the inaugural 1930 FIFA World Cup?
Uruguay won the inaugural 1930 FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina 4–2 in the final in front of 93,000 spectators in Montevideo to become the first nation to win the World Cup.
Who scored the first goal in World Cup history?
The first goal in World Cup history was scored by Lucien Laurent of France, during the 1930 tournament.
Why were the 1942 and 1946 World Cups not held?
The 1942 and 1946 World Cup competitions, which Germany and Brazil sought to host, were cancelled due to World War II.
Which national teams have won the FIFA World Cup?

Eight national teams have won the FIFA World Cup: Brazil with five titles; Germany and Italy with four each; Argentina with three; France and Uruguay with two each; and England and Spain with one each.
Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
Brazil is the most successful World Cup team with five titles, won in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002, and is also the only nation to have played in every World Cup tournament.
Who are the reigning FIFA World Cup champions?
The reigning champions are Argentina, who won their third title at the 2022 World Cup by defeating France.
How many teams compete in the FIFA World Cup final tournament?
As of the 2022 World Cup, 32 teams competed in the final tournament, a format used since 1998. The competition is scheduled to expand to 48 teams starting with the 2026 World Cup.
How many national teams have competed in the World Cup and how many have won it?
In all, 80 national teams have played in at least one World Cup, and the trophy has been won by eight of them.
Tell me about the Jules Rimet Trophy.

The Jules Rimet Trophy was awarded to the World Cup winning team from 1930 to 1970. Originally known simply as the World Cup or Coupe du Monde, it was renamed in 1946 after FIFA president Jules Rimet. Brazil's third victory in 1970 entitled them to keep it permanently, but it was stolen in 1983 and never recovered, apparently melted down by the thieves.
Describe the current FIFA World Cup Trophy.

The current FIFA World Cup Trophy was first awarded in 1974 and was designed by Italian designer Silvio Gazzaniga. It is 36 cm high, made of solid 18 carat gold, weighs 6.175 kg, and its base contains two layers of semi-precious malachite. It is not awarded permanently; winners receive a gold-plated replica afterwards.
When did the World Cup expand from 16 to 24 to 32 teams?
In the tournaments between 1934 and 1978, 16 teams competed in each edition. The tournament was expanded to 24 teams in 1982, and then to 32 teams in 1998.
What format will the 2026 FIFA World Cup use?

On 14 March 2023, FIFA approved a revised format for the 2026 tournament featuring 48 teams in 12 groups of four, with the top eight third-placed teams joining the group winners and runners-up in a new round of 32.
Who will host the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be jointly hosted by Canada, Mexico, and the United States, the first time a World Cup is shared by three host nations, and it will give Mexico the distinction of being the first country to host games in three World Cups.
Which country was the first in Africa to host the World Cup?
South Africa became the first African nation to host the World Cup, staging the tournament in 2010.
Which was the first World Cup held in Asia?
The 2002 FIFA World Cup, hosted jointly by South Korea and Japan, was the first one held in Asia and the first tournament with multiple hosts.
How many times has each confederation hosted the World Cup?
UEFA (Europe) has hosted the World Cup 11 times, CONMEBOL (South America) 5 times, CONCACAF 3 times, AFC (Asia) 2 times, and CAF (Africa) once, while OFC (Oceania) has never hosted the tournament.
When do host nations and defending champions receive automatic qualification?

Host nations have received automatic qualification to the final tournament from the 1938 World Cup onwards. Defending champions were also granted automatic qualification between 1938 and 2002, but this was withdrawn from the 2006 World Cup, requiring the champions to qualify; Brazil, winners in 2002, were the first defending champions to play qualifying matches.
How are points awarded in the World Cup group stage?

Since 1994, three points have been awarded for a win, one for a draw, and none for a loss in the group stage; before that, winners received two points. The top two teams from each group advance to the knockout stage.
Who is the all-time top scorer at the FIFA World Cup?

Miroslav Klose of Germany is the all-time top scorer at the World Cup with 16 goals, scored between 2002 and 2014. He broke Ronaldo of Brazil's record of 15 goals during the 2014 semi-final against Brazil.
Who holds the record for most goals in a single World Cup?
France's Just Fontaine holds the record for most goals scored in a single World Cup, with all 13 of his goals scored in the 1958 tournament.
Which players have appeared in the most World Cups?

Six players share the record for playing in five World Cups: Mexico's Antonio Carbajal, Rafael Márquez, and Andrés Guardado; Germany's Lothar Matthäus; Argentina's Lionel Messi; and Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo, with Ronaldo also being the only player to score in five tournaments.
Who has played the most World Cup matches?
Lionel Messi has played the most World Cup matches overall, with 26 appearances.
Who are the only people to win the World Cup as both player and head coach?

Brazil's Mário Zagallo, West Germany's Franz Beckenbauer, and France's Didier Deschamps are the only people to win the World Cup as both player and head coach. Zagallo won in 1958 and 1962 as a player and 1970 as coach, Beckenbauer in 1974 as captain and 1990 as coach, and Deschamps in 1998 as captain and 2018 as coach.
Which national team has made the most World Cup final appearances?
Germany has appeared in the most World Cup finals, with eight, as well as the most semi-finals (13) and quarter-finals (16). Germany has also made the most top-four finishes, with 13.
How many World Cup titles have European and South American teams won respectively?
European nations have won twelve World Cup titles, while South American nations have won ten. The final has only ever been contested by teams from the UEFA and CONMEBOL confederations.
Which non-European, non-South American teams have reached a World Cup semi-final?
Only three teams from outside Europe and South America have reached a World Cup semi-final: the United States in 1930, South Korea in 2002, and Morocco in 2022.
When was the Golden Ball award first given?
The Golden Ball, awarded for the best player, was first given in 1982. The first recipient was Paolo Rossi of Italy.
When were the Golden Boot, Golden Glove, and Young Player awards first introduced?

The Golden Boot for top goalscorer was first awarded in 1982, the Golden Glove for best goalkeeper (formerly the Lev Yashin Award) was first awarded in 1994, and the FIFA Young Player Award was first awarded in 2006. The FIFA Fair Play Trophy dates back to 1970.
Who won the Golden Boot at the 1958 World Cup and how many goals did they score?
Just Fontaine won the Golden Boot at the 1958 World Cup in Sweden, scoring 13 goals.
Which national team has scored the most goals in World Cup history?
Brazil has scored the most goals in World Cup history, with 237, followed by Germany with 232 and Argentina with 152.
How was the very first World Cup mascot, and when did mascots begin?
Each FIFA World Cup since 1966 has had its own mascot or logo. World Cup Willie, the mascot for the 1966 competition, was the first World Cup mascot.
What was the estimated viewership of recent World Cups?

The viewership of the 2018 World Cup was estimated at 3.57 billion, close to half the global population, while engagement with the 2022 World Cup was estimated at 5 billion, with about 1.5 billion people watching the final match.
Which nations have won consecutive World Cup titles?
Italy (1934 and 1938) and Brazil (1958 and 1962) are the only nations to have won consecutive World Cup titles.
What is the longest streak of World Cups won by teams from a single confederation?
The longest streak of World Cups won by a single confederation is four, with the 2006, 2010, 2014, and 2018 tournaments all won by UEFA teams: Italy, Spain, Germany, and France respectively.
How did host nations perform in their own World Cups?

Six of the eight champions have won one of their titles while playing at home. The exceptions are Brazil, who lost the deciding 1950 match on home soil, and Spain, which reached only the second round at home in 1982. South Africa (2010) and Qatar (2022) failed to advance beyond the first round as hosts.
